prompt:
Capacity review is next week and I want a quick 'where did the space go' rollup for a handful of log volumes. No du on the boxes we care about, and I don't trust hand-rolled loops after last time — write it once, properly.

Build `duroll.ps1` in the repo root: `pwsh -NoProfile -File duroll.ps1 -Root <dir> [-Top <n>]`. One line per directory:

    <relpath> <size>

- Every directory under the root is a row, plus the root itself which prints as `.`. A directory's size is RECURSIVE: the byte sum of every file anywhere beneath it, so a parent is always >= any of its children. Hidden dotfiles count. Directories with nothing in them are 0.
- Rows sorted by raw byte count, biggest first; equal sizes fall back to ordinal byte order of the relpath ('B' before 'a' before 'b'). Print the top N rows; -Top defaults to 5, and anything less than 1 must be rejected by parameter validation, not by an if.
- Sizes are IEC, from pinned math, not any Format-* cmdlet: under 1024 bytes print `<n> B` as a bare integer; under 1024^2 print KiB; everything else prints MiB (we cap there). KiB/MiB get exactly one decimal, invariant culture, and halves round AWAY from zero — 1280 bytes is `1.3 KiB`, not 1.2. Pick the unit from the RAW byte count before any rounding: 1048575 bytes is `1024.0 KiB`, not 1.0 MiB.
- Relative paths use forward slashes on every platform.
- Missing root: stderr `duroll: root not found: <path>`, exit 66, nothing on stdout.

Acceptance: `pwsh -NoProfile -File test_duroll.ps1` from the workspace root must pass unmodified — don't touch the test file.
notes: 6P4 item 2: disk-usage rollup with pinned IEC math. Pins probed 2026-07-16 on pwsh 7.6.3: [math]::Round defaults to banker's rounding (1280/1024 = 1.25 -> 1.2), so the 1.3 KiB expectation forces MidpointRounding::AwayFromZero; unit-from-raw-bytes pin (1048575 -> 1024.0 KiB) forces threshold selection before rounding; tie fixture (B/a/b all 512) forces ordinal tie-break over culture sort; logs/.audit forces -Force enumeration; -Top 0 must fail via parameter validation (ValidateRange probe: binding error, exit 1, no stdout). Differentiation: bash-dureport aggregates du-style text with awk; c-dusweep is a C treewalk — here the surface is FileInfo object enumeration + .NET rounding/threshold math and validation attributes, no text-tool analog. No overlap with ps-oddwalk (item 1): plain names here, per-DIRECTORY recursive sums vs per-file action rules.
